canonical_summary: AgentOS (also called 'the vault') is a personal session memory layer built on top of the ameen-context MCP server, designed so every Claude Code session auto-loads relevant project context at startup and auto-saves findings at close. The core infrastructure is complete: SessionStart and SessionEnd hooks, a `context_save_session` MCP tool, a v1 keyword+cwd-based project classifier, a backfill pipeline for processing historical session transcripts, and a fingerprint guard in session_end.py to block recursive meta-session processing. A major data cleanup pass on 2026-05-25 removed 2,233 garbage sessions and 2,631 stale queue entries, preserving 19 valid pending entries and re-running synthesize_memory.py to rebuild clean project memory from scratch; the MCP server is registered globally and confirmed active. A project registry at data/projects/registry.json tracks 7 registered projects (agentos, rfp-project, rfp_intelligence_pro, RFP_Atharii, and others), with an agent map organized per project with role descriptions in table format; agentos is the infrastructure layer whose agents run across all other projects. A 21/21 test suite confirmed correct registry structure, including that 'vault' and 'vault setup' keywords correctly map to agentos, and that the Workshop_1_Files directory falls back to agentos as the default project — the 'vault' reference in that directory was identified as an Obsidian vault for Building Bridges Workshop 1 materials, not a separate AgentOS project. Next priorities are validating the 19 remaining queue entries in the next synthesis pass and confirming end-to-end hook registration stability via install_hooks.sh.

known_solutions.problem: Memory Vault repo design only activates inside its own directory — not a global layer.
known_solutions.solution: Extend ameen-context MCP (already global via ~/.claude.json) with session memory tools, and wire SessionStart/SessionEnd hooks in ~/.claude/settings.json for auto-load + auto-save.

decision_log.decision: Build session memory as an extension of ameen-context, not as a separate memory-vault repo.
v1 classification is keyword + cwd path matching. LLM-based classification deferred to v2.
v1 SessionEnd hook does best-effort raw capture and queues for v2 LLM structuring. Model can also call context_save_session directly mid-session for high-quality saves.
Use install_hooks.sh as the delivery mechanism for hook registration since direct agent modification is blocked
Use two-tier scoring model with hard gates before weighted scoring
Use two-tier scoring model with hard gates before weighted scoring for RFP eligibility
Use cwd field from JSONL entries as primary signal for project classification
Update registry.json to include DHS Consulting and IDeaS-Lab project paths
Build backfill script to process 15 historical sessions through the v2 structuring pipeline
Do not edit the PPTX file — only read it with python-pptx and apply changes to facilitator markdown docs
Use `claude mcp add <name> -s user <interpreter> <script>` for global MCP registration
MCP server config does not go in settings.json
Use queue-operation pattern for canonical summary updates rather than inline editing
Treat recursive queue operations as valid sessions with summary reflecting their meta-role
Two-tier eligibility scoring approach (hard gates first, then weighted scoring) is the intended design direction for rfp-tooling
Treat recursive queue operations as valid sessions with summary reflecting their meta-role
Treat recursive queue operations as valid sessions with summary reflecting their meta-role
Use queue-operation pattern for canonical summary generation when no existing summary exists
Treat triply-nested recursive queue operations as valid sessions with summary reflecting their meta-role
Use queue-operation pattern for canonical summary updates rather than inline editing
Treat recursive queue operations as valid sessions with summary reflecting their meta-role
Treat recursive queue operations as valid sessions with summary reflecting their meta-role
Treat recursive queue operations as valid sessions with summary reflecting their meta-role
Purge garbage sessions and stale queue entries before re-synthesizing memory
Fingerprint guard in session_end.py is the mechanism to block recursive meta-session processing
Replace existing repo with a clean single-commit repo with no prior history
Keep the repo private going forward
agentos is the infrastructure layer whose agents run across all other projects
Agent map should be organized per project with role descriptions in table format
The SessionStart hook is correctly wired and working; prior session without hook output was opened before the fix was deployed
